title: Projection as secondary index feature not working as expected
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
I don't get any results when writing a query that uses a projection following the syntax introduced in 25.11 - https://presentations.clickhouse.com/2025-release-25.11/#14
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```sql
CREATE TABLE uk_price_paid2
(
price UInt32,
date Date,
postcode1 LowCardinality(String),
postcode2 LowCardinality(String),
type Enum8('terraced' = 1, 'semi-detached' = 2, 'detached' = 3, 'flat' = 4, 'other' = 0),
is_new UInt8,
duration Enum8('freehold' = 1, 'leasehold' = 2, 'unknown' = 0),
addr1 String,
addr2 String,
street LowCardinality(String),
locality LowCardinality(String),
town LowCardinality(String),
district LowCardinality(String),
county LowCardinality(String),
PROJECTION by_time (
-- works as an index for the main table representation
SELECT _part_offset ORDER BY date
)
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
ORDER BY (postcode1, postcode2, addr1, addr2);
```
```sql
INSERT INTO uk_price_paid2 SELECT *
FROM file('uk.parquet')
```
If you then run these two queries, they should return the same results:
```sql
SELECT *
FROM uk_price_paid2
WHERE (date = '2008-11-19') AND (town = 'BARNARD CASTLE')
SETTINGS optimize_use_projections = 1
Query id: a82684fe-1c77-4307-9532-5b1bc7d5e2c8
Ok.
0 rows in set. Elapsed: 0.006 sec.
```
```sql
SELECT *
FROM uk_price_paid2
WHERE (date = '2008-11-19') AND (town = 'BARNARD CASTLE')

key_bug_features: Projection as secondary index not returning expected results
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE uk_price_paid3 (id UInt32, name String, created_at DateTime, status Enum8('active' = 1, 'inactive' = 2), score Float32, tags Array(String), PROJECTION by_status (SELECT _part_offset ORDER BY status) ) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY (id, created_at);
SELECT * FROM uk_price_paid3 WHERE status = 'active' SETTINGS optimize_use_projections = 1;
SELECT * FROM uk_price_paid3 WHERE created_at > '2023-01-01 00:00:00' AND score < 100.0;
